text: Alma Center: Daily IDF update — submarine center destroyed, 2 cruise missile sites hit. Iran missile near Orot Rabin power plant missed. Saudi intercepts 32+ drones targeting Ras Tanura/Abqaiq. Southern Tehran residential strike kills 12. Senate war powers vote fails 47-53 for 3rd time.

Reuters: Hengaw 7th report documents 6,530 killed in Iran (640 civilians). Pentagon signs munitions surge deals with BAE/Lockheed/Honeywell. AP-NORC poll: 59% of Americans say military action excessive. $580M in pre-announcement oil bets under insider trading investigation.

OSINT: IRGC Wave 80 featured four successive missile salvos in 40 minutes — tracking shows Kheibar Shekan, Emad, and Sejjil ballistic missiles used. Wave also targeted US bases at Ali al-Salem/Arifjan (Kuwait), al-Azraq (Jordan), Sheikh Isa (Bahrain). IDF reporting multiple interceptions over Tel Aviv, Hadera, Bnei Brak; debris from one intercept caused vehicle fire in central Tel Aviv.
verificationNote: Confirmed by PressTV, Al Jazeera liveblog, Times of Israel liveblog.

text: IntelCrab: Iran has formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan and issued 5 counter-conditions via Araghchi. Key demand: international recognition of Iran's authority over Strait of Hormuz — this is a non-starter for US/Israel. Iran also demanding Vance replace Witkoff and Kushner in any talks. White House has rejected both demands. Gap between the two sides remains enormous.
verificationNote: PressTV, Anadolu Agency, NPR confirmed the 5-condition rejection. Daily Beast confirmed Vance demand.

text: Alma Center: IDF Day 26 summary — Hezbollah anti-tank commander Hassan Kamal Halawi killed in Qaaqaait al-Jisr drone strike; 5th and final Litani bridge destroyed (Katz confirms all 5 blown). US A-10s strike PMF in Anbar Province (7 killed, 10 wounded). Iraq NSC authorizes PMF self-defense response. Iran names Zolghadr as new SNSC secretary. Saudi Arabia/UAE reportedly moving toward war entry per WSJ.
verificationNote: Naharnet confirmed Halawi killing. Israel Hayom confirmed 5th bridge. Alma Research daily report March 25.

text: NPR: Philippines declares national State of Energy Emergency — first country globally to do so due to Iran war. 98% of oil from Gulf; fuel prices more than doubled. Gasoline reserves 53 days, diesel 46 days, LPG 23.5 days. 4-day work week for government ordered. Japan announces largest-ever strategic oil reserve release (30 days). Asia embracing energy austerity as Hormuz blockade enters Day 23.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Newsweek, UPI, and CNN all confirmed Philippines energy emergency.

text: Analysis: Iranian ballistic missile intercepted over Lebanese airspace for the first time — a significant tactical development. Iranian missiles are now routing through Lebanon's airspace to approach Israel from the north, potentially to avoid radar coverage gaps. IDF investigating two separate David's Sling failures (Dimona/Arad) with no connection found between incidents. Israel now has identified systematic gaps in northern approach corridors.
verificationNote: First Lebanese intercept confirmed by Times of Israel. David's Sling investigation confirmed by Washington Post.

text: UN SG Guterres: Declared the war 'out of control' — 'this has gone too far, the world is staring down the barrel of a wider war.' Appointed veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as personal envoy for the conflict. Called on the US and Israel to end the war and on Iran to stop attacking neighbors. Called a direct US-Iran meeting 'the last opportunity to avoid a fully fledged escalation.'
verificationNote: UN News, Al Jazeera Day 26, and Pravda EN all confirmed Guterres statement and Arnault appointment.

text: CENTCOM confirms 10,000th Iranian target struck — Admiral Cooper video statement says 92% of Iran's largest naval vessels destroyed, 2/3 of missile/drone/naval production facilities damaged or destroyed, and attack pace down 90% since Day 1. B-52s delivering 70,000 lbs of munitions per mission.
verificationNote: Confirmed via CENTCOM official video statement and Times of Israel liveblog.

text: Day 26 Hormuz update: Only 6 AIS-visible transits today (vs 100+/day pre-war). Iran ghost fleet continues active — 19 Iranian tanker loadings confirmed since war start, ~28 million barrels ($2B revenue) flowing to China. 72 ghost fleet tankers anchored off Malaysia (Johor) per UANI. Saudi Petroline pipeline at capacity. Insurance premiums remain 5-7.5% per voyage.
verificationNote: Confirmed via UANI shipping tracker and UKMTO reports.

text: Saudi Arabia confirmed granting US access to King Fahd Air Base — reversal of earlier refusal. UAE in parallel discussions on troop deployment. Both Gulf nations reportedly discouraging Trump from ceasefire until Iran military capabilities further degraded. Saudi FM warns 'patience not unlimited.' If Saudi/UAE formally enter, conflict scope expands dramatically — additional staging, air power, and regional legitimacy for coalition.
verificationNote: Confirmed via Bloomberg, WSJ, i24 News reporting.

text: Hezbollah anti-tank missile unit commander Hassan Kamal Halawi killed in Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh (Qaaqaait al-Jisr). He commanded all anti-tank operations on Hezbollah's southern front. Separately, Hezbollah launched 10 rockets at Haifa Bay — rocket launcher destroyed by IAF minutes after launch. One IDF reservist seriously wounded, battalion commander lightly wounded. IDF reports ~700 Hezbollah fighters killed since Lebanon front opened.
verificationNote: Confirmed via IDF announcement, Times of Israel, Tribune India.

text: Day 26 assessment: Iranian ballistic missile intercepted over Lebanon for first time — suggests Iran routing missiles via Lebanese airspace to approach from north and bypass primary Arrow-3 coverage from south. IRGC Wave 80 used 4 successive salvos in 40 minutes — testing saturation tactics. US-Iran diplomatic standoff: 15-point US plan rejected; Iran issues 5 counter-conditions including Hormuz sovereignty recognition. Saudi/UAE war entry movement is the most significant development of Day 26.
verificationNote: CTP-ISW Day 26 assessment based on open-source reporting.

text: Houthi ballistic missile strikes Israeli-owned tanker Scarlet Ray in northern Red Sea near Yanbu — UKMTO confirmed. 22nd confirmed Houthi maritime strike since conflict began. Kuwait airport fuel tank hit by Iranian drones; fire contained, no casualties. Six Hezbollah-linked plotters arrested in Kuwait for alleged assassination plot — same day as airport attack, likely coordinated pressure on Kuwait to deter its continued hosting of US forces.
verificationNote: Houthi claim confirmed by UKMTO. Kuwait airport fire confirmed by Al Arabiya and Kuwait civil aviation authority.

text: Day 26 PM summary: Brent crude dropped ~$2.72 to $99.75/bbl (from $102.47 previous close) on Trump peace-talk signals — first time Brent under $100 since early this week. WTI at $90.32 (-2.2%). Gas still $3.977/gal national avg — pump prices lag oil by 2-3 days. Iran's formal rejection of 15-point plan with 5 counter-conditions means no ceasefire imminent. CENTCOM hit 10,000 Iranian targets. Saudi/UAE closer to war entry than ever before.
verificationNote: Compiled from multiple open-source reporting streams and official statements.

text: sentdefender: CONFIRMED — Israel killed IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri in overnight strike on Bandar Abbas naval HQ. Multiple senior aides also killed. Tangsiri had commanded the IRGC Navy since 2018 and is the principal architect of the Strait of Hormuz mining operation. IDF: 'The man directly responsible for the Hormuz chokehold was blown up and eliminated.'
verificationNote: Confirmed by IDF/Defense Minister Katz statement. Reported by Washington Post, Al Jazeera.

text: OSINTdefender: Iran launched ballistic missiles at UAE on Day 27 — 2 killed in Abu Dhabi by falling debris from intercepted missile. Saudi Arabia intercepted a drone in the Eastern Province. Kuwait and Bahrain also attacked. GCC Secretary-General al-Budaiwi: Iranian actions 'overcome all red lines.' Gulf states formally protesting at the UN Security Council.
verificationNote: UAE attack confirmed by Times of Israel Day 27 liveblog and Al Jazeera.

text: GeoConfirmed: IRGC Wave 81 'True Promise 4' — geolocated strike footage confirms hits near Haifa port area, Netanya, and outskirts of Bnei Brak. 12 injured in Bnei Brak from cluster munition debris. Iran fired 7 separate missile salvos at central Israel during Day 27 — saturation tactics designed to overwhelm interception systems. Khorramshahr-4 and Emad ballistic missiles confirmed in footage.
verificationNote: Based on open-source geolocation of verified footage and IDF statements.

text: CTP-ISW Day 27 assessment: Pentagon 'final blow' options including Kharg Island seizure signal Trump considering ground phase. Iran's fortification of Kharg (mines, MANPADs, additional troops) suggests they've anticipated this move for days. Iraq's formal authorization of PMF counter-strikes against US aircraft is the most significant escalation in the Iraq theater since the war began — potential for US combat casualties to rise sharply.
verificationNote: CTP-ISW Day 27 assessment based on open-source reporting from Axios, CNN, Al Jazeera.

text: Conflicts Watcher: Day 27 economic summary: Brent recovering to ~$97/bbl after dropping to $93 on peace-talk hopes then rebounding on Iran's firm rejection. WTI ~$91.64. AAA gas $3.981/gal nationally — $0.019 from $4.00 milestone. Hormuz selective blockade shift: Iran says 'non-hostile' ships may transit; Japan explicitly approved. This is a tactical shift, not a reopening — fewer than 10 transits/day vs 138 pre-war.
verificationNote: Compiled from AAA, FX Leaders oil forecast, Al Jazeera Hormuz reporting.

text: IntelSlavaZ: Staff Sgt. Ori Greenberg (21, Golani Brigade recon) killed in Lebanon gunfight — 3rd IDF soldier killed in Lebanon. IDF confirms all 6 Litani River bridges now destroyed; Hezbollah southern forces cut off from Bekaa Valley resupply. Golani Brigade continuing sweep south of Litani. Trump Truth Social warning to Iran: 'NO TURNING BACK' — responding to Araghchi's flat rejection of 15-point plan.
verificationNote: Greenberg killing confirmed by Haaretz, IDF. Trump Truth Social post confirmed by NBC News.
